hours_formula_detail: Alabama applies the flat federal ABAWD work standard: 80 hours per month (averaging 20 hours/week). Unpaid community service / volunteering counts toward those 80 hours as a qualifying work activity — it is NOT a separate allotment-divided-by-minimum-wage workfare formula for general volunteering. The DHR Points of Eligibility manual (Chapter 17) states ABAWDs must meet the '20 hour/week work requirement (80 hours a month)'. Chapter 7, Section 710A lists 'performing beneficial and verifiable unpaid volunteer work for a program or project which serves a useful public purpose' as a qualifying activity counted at actual hours toward the 80-hour standard. Alabama's separate optional workfare program (where assigned hours = allotment / minimum wage) is distinct and not the general volunteer lane. OBBBA/HR1 (P.L. 119-21, 2025) did not change the 80-hour figure — it expanded the population subject to the requirement (age 18-64, dependent exemption only if youngest child under 14, removal of vet/homeless/former-foster exemptions) and curtailed waivers.
minimum_wage_detail: Not used in the hours computation because Alabama uses the flat 80-hour standard, not an allotment/minimum-wage formula for general community service. Alabama has no state minimum wage statute, so the federal floor of $7.25/hr applies in the state for any context that references minimum wage (e.g., the optional workfare program). Per instructions, minimumWageUsd is set to 0 for a federal_80 classification.
qualifying_org_definition: Any program or project serving a useful public purpose. Chapter 7, Section 710A explicitly includes health, social service, environmental protection, education, urban or rural development, welfare, recreation, public safety, or daycare — and even work for friends or relatives. No 501(c)(3) or nonprofit registration requirement is stated in the policy; the activity must be beneficial, verifiable, and unpaid. The Alabama Legal Help recipient-facing explainer (sourced from DHR policy) corroborates: 'Unpaid community service for a program doing public service in such fields as health, social service and day care. Many churches have programs that meet this requirement.'
qualifyingOrgShort: Any program or project serving a useful public purpose (health, social service, environmental, education, welfare, recreation, public safety, daycare, even work for friends/relatives); no 501(c)(3) or registration requirement — the unpaid work must simply be beneficial and verifiable.
accepts_outofstate_detail: Alabama policy (Chapter 7, Section 710A) defines qualifying volunteer work by activity type and public purpose, with no requirement that the organization be in-state, registered, or hold 501(c)(3) status. The policy is silent on org location. The federal default (7 CFR 273.24 / 273.7) does not restrict org location; absent a state restriction, out-of-state 501(c)(3)s should be accepted operationally, but no source affirmatively states this — hence 'silent'.
geographic_waivers: None — fully enforced statewide. Alabama has no active ABAWD waivers in FY2026 (Oct 2025-Sep 2026). The FY2026 SNAP E&T State Plan confirmed anticipated ABAWDs in waived areas = 0. Post-OBBBA (signed July 4, 2025), OBBBA severely curtailed states' ability to obtain area waivers and prior nationwide waivers expired/terminated by November 2025; Alabama had none previously.
geographicWaivers: None active in FY2026. Alabama is fully enforced statewide with zero geographic/county ABAWD waivers; FY2026 State Plan reports anticipated ABAWDs in waived areas = 0. OBBBA (2025) further restricted area-waiver eligibility nationwide.
submissionMechanism: Documentation of work/volunteer hours is provided to the DHR eligibility worker (no named org-facing form). Reportable changes and periodic reports are submitted via county office (in person), mail, fax, or the MyAlabama.gov / MyDHR online portal.
reportingCadence: Change-triggered reporting on a 4-month ABAWD certification cycle. Changes in work/volunteer hours that affect ABAWD compliance must be reported by the 10th of the month after the change; a periodic (six-month) report applies to longer certification periods. ABAWDs are certified in 4-month periods.
submission_deadline: Changes in work hours must be reported by the 10th of the month after the month in which the change occurred (Chapter 17, Section 1700). The six-month report form (for income/household changes) must be returned between the 1st and 10th of the sixth month of the certification period. ABAWDs have a 4-month certification period.
submissionDeadline: Changes in work/volunteer hours reported by the 10th of the month following the change (Chapter 17, Section 1700). Periodic report due between the 1st and 10th of the report month. ABAWD certification period is 4 months.
notes:
VERIFICATION PASS (2026-06-22, FY2026/OBBBA): Confirmed the prior HIGH-confidence finding. Classification is federal_80 — Alabama applies the flat 80-hour/month (20 hr/wk) federal ABAWD standard, and unpaid community service/volunteering counts toward those 80 hours at actual hours (NOT an allotment/minimum-wage workfare formula for general volunteering). Web verification corroborated every key field: (a) 80 hours/month confirmed by Alabama Legal Help recipient explainer ('at least 80 hours per month'), Ballotpedia, Propel 2026 guide, USDA FNA work-requirements page; (b) broad qualifying-org definition with no registration/501(c)(3) requirement confirmed by Chapter 7 Section 710A (extracted in prior pass) and Alabama Legal Help ('Unpaid community service for a program doing public service in such fields as health, social service and day care. Many churches have programs that meet this requirement'); (c) no active ABAWD waivers and statewide enforcement in FY2026 confirmed by FY2026 State Plan, Ballotpedia, and multiple secondary sources; (d) OBBBA/HR1 (P.L. 119-21, signed July 4, 2025) did NOT change the 80-hour figure — it expanded the subject population (age to 64, dependent exemption only if youngest child under 14, removal of veteran/homeless/former-foster exemptions) and curtailed waivers (per NACo HR1 guidance summary and CRS R48755). No source named a volunteer-hour verification form (verification_instrument = unknown); no source addressed remote/virtual volunteering (remote_stance = silent, a downgrade from prior 'unknown' to the schema's allowed value); no source addressed out-of-state nonprofits (silent). County variation = false (state-administered, uniform policy). The DHR-FAP-1942 ABAWD notice PDF and the USDA FNA waiver page could not be text-extracted via WebFetch this pass (binary/timeout), but their content is corroborated by readable secondary sources and the prior primary-source extractions. Confidence retained at HIGH: primary policy authority (Chapter 7 Section 710A + Chapter 17 + FY2026 State Plan, all directly read in the prior FINAL/EXTENDED passes) directly confirms classification + hours + qualifying-org shape, and FY2026/OBBBA web sources are fully consistent.

--- PRIOR-PASS HISTORY (preserved) ---
FINAL PASS (2026-06-21): Extracted full text from DHR Points of Eligibility Chapter 7 (17 pages) and FY2026 SNAP E&T State Plan (178 pages). Chapter 7, Section 710A quoted: '(3) Performing beneficial and verifiable unpaid volunteer work for a program or project which serves a useful public purpose; such volunteer work may include but is not limited to providing improvements to public facilities or performing work in fields such as health, social service, environmental protection, education, urban or rural development, welfare, recreation, public safety or daycare including work for friends or relatives.' No restriction to 501(c)(3)s, approved site lists, or formal org registration; no EDP, case-manager assignment, or pre-clearance before volunteer hours count. Section 702 names DHR-WR-1802A for work registration only, NOT for volunteer-hour documentation. State Plan confirms state-level administration, voluntary A-RESET (not a mandatory ABAWD gateway), and no county-level waivers (anticipated ABAWDs in waived areas = 0).
EXTENDED FIELDS PASS (2026-06-21): Chapter 17 POE manual confirms '80 hours a month' verbatim (federal_80). Out-of-state nonprofit: silent. Geographic waivers: none, statewide enforcement. Submission UX: four channels (county office in person, mail, fax, MyAlabama.gov online portal) for the periodic report; ABAWD hour changes reported by the 10th of the month after the change; 4-month certification period; reporting cadence = 'other' (change-triggered on a 4-month cycle). No named state form for volunteer verification.
REFINEMENT PASS (2026-06-21): Adversarial search across six angles (mandatory work registration before volunteer hours; EDP/case-manager prerequisite; approved-site list / nonprofit MOU; A-RESET as mandatory gateway; county variation; hidden gating in legal-aid explainers). Zero evidence of a mandatory EDP, state-imposed case-manager assignment, pre-approved site list, or nonprofit registration requirement. A-RESET confirmed voluntary everywhere it appears.
conflicts_with_prior: remote_stance changed from prior value 'unknown' to schema-conformant 'silent' (no source addresses remote/virtual volunteering — same underlying finding, normalized to allowed enum value).

Classification surfaced explicitly as 'federal_80' (prior file had hours_formula='federal_80' but did not carry the high-stakes 'classification' field); affirmatively confirmed Alabama does NOT use an allotment/minimum-wage formula for general community-service volunteering.
